generated_display: July 8, 2026 (seed data — refreshes automatically every 6 hours)

items.title: Anthropic unveils Claude Science Workbench, connecting Opus 4.8 to 60+ scientific databases
items.summary: The workbench links Claude to genomics, proteomics, cheminformatics, and clinical-trial databases, pushing AI deeper into scientific research workflows.

items.title: Anthropic overtakes OpenAI on self-reported revenue, targeting $47B and profitability by 2029
items.summary: Anthropic says it is on course to reach $47 billion in revenue and profitability a year ahead of OpenAI, reshaping the competitive landscape among leading labs.

items.title: Google DeepMind delays Gemini 3.5 Pro to July 17 for a full architectural rebuild
items.summary: DeepMind scraps the Gemini 2.5 Pro architecture for a ground-up redesign adding a 2M-token context window, a Deep Think reasoning layer, and autonomous workflow capabilities.

items.title: Sam Altman seeks a 'new world order' for AI as OpenAI loses ground to Google and Anthropic
items.summary: With rivals gaining, OpenAI floats giving Washington a 5% equity stake, raising governance and oversight questions about the state's role in frontier AI.

items.title: Commerce Department lifts export controls; Anthropic restores Fable 5 and Mythos 5 globally with new safeguards
items.summary: After US export controls were lifted, Anthropic brought the models back online globally, adding cybersecurity classifiers as a safety guardrail.

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 with near-Opus performance at $2/$10 introductory pricing
items.summary: Sonnet 5 lands close to Opus 4.8 quality at aggressive introductory pricing through August 31, intensifying the price-performance race among frontier labs.

items.title: Unisound U2: a 266B-parameter MoE built for agents hits 72.2% on SWE-bench Verified
items.summary: The mixture-of-experts model (266B total / 10B active) posts strong reasoning and coding scores, underscoring the shift toward agent-optimized architectures.

items.title: OpenThoughts-Agent: data recipes for agentic models
items.summary: The paper details reproducible data recipes for training agentic models, part of a fast-growing body of work on tool use and long-horizon reasoning.

items.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to Agentic AI: from foundations to systems
items.summary: A broad survey of agentic AI covering foundations, memory, evaluation, and multi-agent system design as autonomy becomes a dominant research theme.

items.title: DeepResearch Bench: a comprehensive benchmark for deep research agents
items.summary: A new benchmark evaluates autonomous research agents on multi-step tasks such as web browsing, tool use, and synthesis, sharpening how the field measures agentic reasoning.

items.title: Topical phase transitions in AI research: an early-warning signature for emerging topics
items.summary: Large-scale analysis finds 'reasoning' grew 4.6x in 2025 and identifies an early-warning signature for emerging AI research topics before they surge.

items.title: Verified Critical Step Optimization for LLM agents
items.summary: A method that targets the highest-impact steps in an agent's trajectory, improving reliability on complex multi-step tasks.

items.title: Left to self-police, AI companies weaken safety commitments, Future of Life study finds
items.summary: A Future of Life Institute study finds leading labs moving the goalposts on safety commitments, with none scoring above a C+ on alignment and accountability.

items.title: Google, Anthropic and OpenAI lead the AI Safety Index; SpaceXAI receives an F
items.summary: The latest AI Safety Index ranks the major labs on transparency, risk management, and governance, with wide gaps between leaders and laggards.

items.title: AI News Today, July 6 2026: the 15 biggest stories
items.summary: A practitioner roundup of the week's most-discussed releases, tools, and debates across the AI community.

items.title: In Case You Missed It: Last Week in AI, June 29 to July 5, 2026
items.summary: A weekly digest summarizing model releases, research, and industry moves for machine-learning practitioners.

items.title: Nvidia tops $40 billion in equity bets on AI startups in 2026
items.summary: Nvidia's expanding investment portfolio deepens its influence across the AI supply chain, raising questions about market concentration.

items.title: Nvidia to let AI startups use its chips now and pay with a share of future revenue
items.summary: Nvidia positions itself as a financier of the AI buildout, with cloud partners reselling chip access to startups in exchange for a cut of future revenue.

items.title: AI chip startups near record funding, raising $8.3B to challenge Nvidia as focus shifts to inference
items.summary: Investors pour billions into inference-focused silicon, betting the next phase of the AI boom rewards efficiency over raw training scale.

items.title: Semiconductor startup funding runs hot: MatX raises $500M, Ayar Labs $500M
items.summary: Custom-silicon and optical-interconnect startups pull in half-billion-dollar rounds as the compute buildout accelerates.

items.title: EU AI Act high-risk enforcement begins August 2, 2026
items.summary: The EU AI Act's obligations for high-risk systems take effect, raising compliance, transparency, and governance stakes for AI deployers worldwide.

items.title: AI copyright: six key rulings shaping 2026 litigation
items.summary: A review of pivotal fair-use and infringement rulings that are setting the rules for how AI systems can lawfully learn from copyrighted work.

items.title: Court orders OpenAI to produce all 20 million output logs in copyright litigation
items.summary: A discovery ruling forces disclosure of millions of model outputs, testing privacy and accountability questions in AI copyright cases.

items.title: Bartz v. Anthropic settles for $1.5B over pirated books, roughly $3,000 per work
items.summary: A landmark copyright settlement: courts held that training on books can be fair use, but storing pirated copies is not, reshaping how training data is sourced.

items.title: France's competition authority fines Google 250M euros for using news articles to train Gemini
items.summary: Regulators penalize Google over unlicensed use of publishers' content, a copyright and competition flashpoint for how models are trained.
